QF.id: VI Public Offering and Corporate Actions_15

question.text: In case of a rights issue of a listed company, in which ISIN are the rights shares credited upon allotment?

answer.text: A new temporary ISIN which is frozen for debit
The existing ISIN of the company
The ISIN of the Rights Entitlements

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rights shares are credited in a new (temporary) ISIN of the company which is frozen for debit, so securities cannot be transferred till available for trading. **The Trap:** The existing ISIN seems logical but a temporary ISIN is used to prevent trading before listing. **Cross-Exam Context:** Temporary ISINs in corporate actions are tested in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The temporary ISIN ensures no illegal trading occurs before shares are listed — it will be merged with the regular ISIN once trading commences.
